inputs.label: Your industry
inputs.description: Used to find relevant news and trends

inputs.label: Topics to monitor
inputs.description: Topics you want tracked, one per line
inputs.placeholder:
AI agents
SaaS pricing
remote work trends

inputs.label: News sources
inputs.description: Specific sites to check for news, one per line

inputs.label: Competitors
inputs.description: Competitor names or URLs to monitor, one per line

inputs.label: Morning briefing time
inputs.description: When to deliver your morning briefing (24h format)

inputs.label: Briefing depth
inputs.description: How detailed your briefings should be

inputs.label: Include today's calendar
inputs.description: Pull in your calendar events for the day

inputs.label: Include email summary
inputs.description: Summarize overnight emails in the briefing

morning-briefing.description: Full morning briefing: overnight news, calendar preview, email summary, and action items

activities.intent: Scan news sources and social platforms for industry topics that changed overnight
steps: Scan configured news_sources for articles matching the user's topics from the last 24 hours
Check X/Reddit for trending discussions related to the user's industry and topics
Check competitor websites and social profiles for any new announcements or changes
Rank findings by significance — filter out routine noise

activities.intent: Pull today's calendar events and flag anything that needs prep
steps: Read today's calendar events via Google Workspace
Identify meetings that need preparation (board meetings, client calls, interviews)
Note any conflicts or back-to-back blocks
Flag deadlines or deliverables due today

activities.intent: Summarize overnight emails and flag anything urgent
steps: Read unread emails received since the last briefing via Google Workspace
Categorize: action required, FYI, scheduling, can wait
Flag emails from VIPs or with urgent language
Count totals: how many new, how many need attention

activities.intent: Compile all findings into a structured morning briefing
steps: Lead with the single most important change or event
Structure the briefing: What Happened > What's On Your Plate > What Needs Attention
Adapt depth to the user's briefing_depth setting (headlines-only, summary, or detailed)
End with a clear action items list if any exist
Store briefing in memory for evening-recap comparison

breaking-alert.description: Quick scan for significant breaking news — only alerts if something genuinely important happened

activities.intent: Fast check of key topics for breaking developments worth interrupting the user
steps: Quick scan of top news sources and X for the user's key topics
Check for major competitor announcements, funding rounds, or product launches
Compare against what was already covered in the morning briefing
Only alert if something significant broke — major news, competitor announcement, or viral mention
Do NOT alert for routine updates. This is interrupt-driven. Silence is fine.

evening-recap.description: End-of-day summary: what happened today, what's tomorrow, anything that fell through the cracks

activities.intent: Summarize the day and preview tomorrow
steps: Summarize what happened today: meetings completed, emails sent, any breaking alerts that fired
Check calendar for tomorrow: what's coming up, any deadlines
Flag anything from this morning's briefing that may not have been addressed
Keep it short — the user is winding down, not ramping up

weekly-trends.description: Monday morning deep dive: what changed this week in your industry, competitor activity, and trend analysis

activities.intent: Deep research on monitored topics to identify weekly trends
steps: Research each monitored topic: what changed this week vs last week
Scan competitor activity: site changes, social posts, news mentions, product updates
Identify trends: what's growing in attention, what's fading, what's new this week
Check if any of the user's topics are trending on social or news platforms

activities.intent: Compile weekly trends into an actionable report
steps: Structure the report: Top Story > Industry Trends > Competitor Activity > What's New
Use week-over-week comparisons wherever possible
Highlight opportunities: trends the user could act on
Keep it strategic — this is a thinking document, not a news dump
Store in memory for next week's comparison

custom-brief.description: On-demand briefing: user asks 'brief me on [topic]' and gets a concise summary with sources

activities.intent: Quick research on a user-specified topic and deliver a concise briefing
steps: Research the user's requested topic via browser and web scraper
Check news, social platforms, and relevant sites for recent developments
Deliver a concise summary: what's happening, why it matters, key sources
Adapt depth to the user's briefing_depth setting
Always cite sources so the user can dig deeper
